questions.question: What is the primary psychological cause of sales resistance in founders?

options.text: Not having a good enough script

questions.explanation: Resistance often stems from the conflict between your identity as a 'builder/helper' and the negative stereotype of a 'seller,' creating cognitive dissonance.

questions.question: Which of these is a common 'avoidance pattern' for technical founders?
questions.explanation: 'Procrastibuilding' is the act of adding more features or refining tech to delay the 'moment of truth' involved in selling.

questions.question: Why does 'just be confident' advice often fail for founders?

options.text: The brain recognizes it as 'faking it' against one's identity

questions.explanation: Your brain values integrity. Trying to 'act' confident when you feel out of alignment triggers an 'imposter' signal.

questions.question: What is the 'good news' about sales resistance?

options.text: It means you don't have to sell
It is proof of your integrity and desire not to manipulate
It helps you save money on CRM tools

questions.explanation: Resistance signals that you care about being honest and helpful. These traits are actually assets when reframed correctly.

questions.question: Instead of 'pitching,' what is a more effective frame for a founder sales call?
questions.explanation: Framing the conversation as 'diagnosis' or 'research' aligns with the founder's problem-solving identity and lowers pressure.

title: Why Your Brain Sabotages Sales - Quiz
